problem.title: Sum Root to Leaf Numbers
problem.description: You are given the root of a binary tree containing digits from 0 to 9 only.
assumptions: Each root-to-leaf path represents a number.
Return sum of all such numbers.

example.explanation: Root-to-leaf numbers: 12 and 13. Sum = 25. DFS passing current number as 10*cur + node.val.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – DFS [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: A depth-first traversal carries a running number formed by appending each node's digit (current = current × 10 + node.val); when a leaf is reached, that number is one complete root-to-leaf value that gets added to the total. Passing the accumulated sum down rather than up avoids the need to store any intermediate path. The key insight is that shifting left by one decimal place (×10) before adding the next digit correctly reconstructs the number encoded by each root-to-leaf path.
